validation_gates.reason: Repository identity, permissions, latest base, target blob, direct helper modules, entrypoint consumers, tests, Makefile, workflows, dependency metadata, package extraction candidate, Directory Rules, PR contract, and receipt schema were inspected.

Main advanced during authoring from 7bf6f95e531300dd421a0e871fdba7a48f5485e6 to 42e28cb1c17be943da70576f427563c7cbd27898 through an unrelated tests/pipelines README change; the target blob remained 39eba24a5e5bfd5943c3f2f3ae69ca3102011b37 before mutation.

The README remains under tools/validators/_common as shared validator implementation documentation and routes schemas, contracts, policy, fixtures, tests, evidence, receipts, release, and public serving to their owning roots.
README.md, jsonschema_runner.py, local_resolver.py, and run_all.py were verified and classified by responsibility.
A bounded exact-import search surfaced seventeen validator scripts and two test modules; Makefile schemas and both schema-validation workflows were inspected.

Registry scan, $id skip/duplicate behavior, Draft 2020-12 construction, file validation, fixture polarity, console output, exit codes, aggregate fail-fast behavior, working-directory assumptions, and known unreachable/output-conflict branches were derived directly from code.
Schema resolution and validation remain subordinate to semantic contracts, policy, evidence, review, release, correction, and rollback; no validator success is represented as public truth.
The working tools implementation and placeholder packages/schema-registry namespace are surfaced without creating dual implementation authority or selecting a migration by assertion.

One H1, balanced fenced blocks, 45 resolved internal links, tables, API contracts, known gaps, definition of done, verification register, maintenance, correction, migration, rollback, and evidence ledger were checked.

No private-key block or common GitHub, AWS, or OpenAI token pattern, credentials, private endpoint, exact sensitive location, living-person data, DNA material, infrastructure detail, or reconstruction hint was introduced.
GitHub returned README blob 12df3198498356b32bf309a314eb255604b37415, matching the locally computed Git blob for the validated content.

Only documentation and its provenance receipt change. No helper code, validator, schema, contract, policy, fixture, test, workflow, package, lifecycle object, release record, runtime, or public artifact is modified.

Required fields, allowed properties, hash patterns, truth-label and validation enums, human-review state, timestamps, citations, artifact path/hash closure, and notes length were checked against the fetched schema.
Documentation-only change; no validator implementation, dependency, fixture, test, workflow, schema, or runtime behavior changed.
Repository checks are observed after draft pull-request creation and were unavailable at receipt emission time.

notes: AI-authored documentation-only update on a scoped review branch.
